articles.title: Election Commission releases final data: 4.88 crore people voted in Tamil Nadu
articles.description: Tamil Nadu witnessed a record 85.1% voter turnout in the recent assembly elections, with 4.88 crore citizens casting their ballots. Women voters significantly outnumbered men, continuing a trend from previous years. The Election Commission faced delays in releasing data but assured robust security and transparency for the upcoming vote counting on May 4.

Both parties raised concerns about the integrity of the storage facilities.

articles.title: As talks stretch, Trump says 'prefer not' to strike Iran again
articles.description: US President Donald Trump voiced frustration over Iran negotiations. He stopped short of threatening new airstrikes. Trump stated he was not satisfied with the current deal. Iran's foreign minister said Tehran is ready for diplomacy if US demands change. The Strait of Hormuz remains a key point of contention. US gasoline prices have surged due to the ongoing situation.

articles.title: China CRUSHES Trump’s ‘ILLEGAL’ Iran Oil Sanctions, ‘Won’t Be Dictated’ | Watch
articles.description: China has pushed back hard against US pressure, moving to block sanctions on its refiners accused of buying Iranian oil. Calling the measures “illegal” and against international norms, Beijing’s Commerce Ministry issued an injunction to shield key companies from Washington’s crackdown. The move signals a growing rift between the world’s two biggest economies, as the battle over Iran’s oil trade intensifies. With Chinese “teapot” refineries under strain and US sanctions tightening, the standoff raises bigger questions about who controls global energy flows—and whether America’s pressure tactics are losing impact.

articles.title: Babar Azam Reacts Strongly To ‘Personal Attacks’ By 90s Legends: 'Players Have Self Respect'
articles.description: Babar Azam addressed criticism from former cricketers, acknowledging that scrutiny is inherent in cricket but emphasizing that personal attacks have become excessive.

articles.title: 'Not presidential': Trump REVEALS Melania 'hates' his dancing to 'gay national anthem'
articles.description: Donald Trump revealed Friday that First Lady Melania Trump “hates” when he dances to the Village People‘s “YMCA.” She “hates when I dance to what’s sometimes referred to as ‘the gay national anthem,’ you know that,” he told a crowd at Florida’s The Villages retirement community Friday.

articles.title: ‘U.S. NAVY PIRATES!’: Trump ‘Publicly Humiliates’ Own Troops In Mideast, Echoes Iran TAUNTS | Watch
articles.description: US President Donald Trump has sparked controversy after comparing US Navy operations in Mideast to “pirates” during a rally in Florida. Speaking about actions amid the blockade of Iranian ports, Trump openly described seizing ships, cargo, and oil, calling it a “very profitable business.” His remarks, delivered to a cheering crowd, have raised serious questions about US strategy in the region and the optics of such aggressive maritime tactics. Is this rhetoric or a reflection of policy? Watch

articles.title: Fish, Film Stars, Singer Zubeen & Pinarayi: 4 Uniques of This Election | I Witness
articles.description: The 2026 state assembly elections, from West Bengal, Kerala, Tamil Nadu, Assam, Puducherry, threw up a campaign season unlike any in recent memory, each state bringing something entirely its own to the table. In Bengal, the discourse took a decisively ‘fishy’ turn, with fish deployed as political bait and food habits becoming a potent marker in the contest over Bengali identity. BJP candidates were seen campaigning while cradling large katla fish, trying to convince voters the party had no intention of interfering with their dinner plates - a spectacle that drew as much ridicule as it did attention. Up in Assam, the campaign was haunted by grief: the mysterious death of Zubeen Garg, described across communities as the “cultural heartbeat of Assam”, cast a long shadow, with parties sparring over who would deliver justice for the beloved singer. And in Kerala, it was the weight of one man’s brand. In Tamil Nadu, after decades of a 2-party fight, an actor making his debut on the political stage is turning out to be quite the X-factor.

articles.title: Election Commission Orders Complete Repoll in Falta After Clashes, Allegations of Vote Tampering
articles.description: The Election Commission has ordered a rare full repoll in West Bengal’s Falta constituency after violence and allegations of electoral malpractice disrupted voting. With all 285 booths set to vote again on May 21, the move highlights serious concerns over electoral integrity ahead of counting.

articles.title: Iran Defies US Warships? Satellite Images Show 'OIL TANKERS LOADING' At Kharg; 'NOTHING CAN STOP...'
articles.description: Fresh satellite imagery is challenging U.S. claims that Iran’s oil system is collapsing under pressure. Images from Kharg Island — the backbone of Tehran’s crude exports — show multiple tankers actively loading oil, signaling that operations are still underway despite the naval blockade. According to Bloomberg, the number of vessels anchored near the island has surged, suggesting Iran has accelerated loading activity since the blockade began. The visuals directly contradict statements by U.S. President Donald Trump, who claimed Iran’s oil network was “choking.”

articles.title: ‘THOUSANDS ON STREETS’: Europe Spirals Into Chaos As Violent Clashes, Fires & Arrests Grip Capitals
articles.description: Europe’s streets are a battlefield this May Day. From Paris to Berlin, International Workers’ Day has transformed into a massive anti-government uprising. Protesters are burning effigies of Emmanuel Macron and clashing with riot police in the German capital, fueled by a toxic mix of high energy prices and anger over the U.S.-led blockade in the Gulf. As labor unions demand wage hikes to counter runaway inflation, the 2026 protests suggest a continent reaching its breaking point.

articles.title: Shilpa Rao addresses AR Rahman communal remark row: You have to abstain as much as possible from being gullible'
articles.description: Playback singer Shilpa Rao has responded to AR Rahman's comments on alleged communal influences in Bollywood. Rao emphasized the importance of artistic authenticity, stating that artists might sacrifice projects to stay true to their creative instincts. She believes choosing personal expression over safe, conventional work, even with potential failure, is crucial for self-representation.
